programs.title: China Untapped: Nanjing 3
programs.desc: Join us in Nanjing’s bustling old quarter, where you can try on silk dresses worth their weight in gold, and see how traditional handicrafts have been given modern twists. We’ll feast on street snacks and local delicacies by the old Confucian Temple, before taking a pleasure cruise along Nanjing’s mother river to round off our exploration of this vibrant and historic city.

programs.title: Nyingchi Ep.1
programs.desc: Join Tianran He in Nyingchi, Xizang Autonomous Region. Fed by the Yarlung Zangbo River and abundant rainfall, this lush subtropical region is home to some of Xizan’g most unique scenery. From sacred mountains and ancient monasteries, to cruising along Nyingchi’s mother river, wandering alpine meadows and savoring stone pot chicken – a beloved local delicacy, Tianran uncovers the beauty of Nyingchi.

programs.title: Basketball dreams soar in Liangshan
programs.desc: Meet basketball girl Aguolieha, who comes from a rural village in the mountainous Liangshan Yi Autonomous Prefecture in southwest China. Playing for a local team called the Blackhawks, the 13-year-old has a big dream: to become a professional player one day and join Team China. At first, her father was not supportive as he feared that sports training would make her grades fall. Proving to be excellent both academically and on the court, she often looks up to her idol Stephen Curry, and trains hard with her peers. She believes that the sky's the limit and that basketball games will help her dreams soar.

programs.title: The sound of casting
programs.desc: For centuries, the fires of craftsmanship have never faded in Pingyao. In this UNESCO World Heritage city once famed for trade, three ancient metalworking traditions — sword casting, gold and silver crafting, and iron forging — continue to thrive. This episode steps into the workshops where artisans shape metal with fire and devotion. Each blade’s edge, each ornament’s gleam, and each curve of wrought iron tells a story of resilience and artistry — revealing how the spirit of Pingyao’s craftsmanship still glows in the heart of time.

programs.title: Indigo Dreams: A Hue of Dali Tie-dye
programs.desc: Dali Tie-dye is the unique textile art of the Bai people, renowned for its exquisite hand-tying and traditional indigo dyeing techniques that create brilliant, one-of-a-kind patterns. Explore the cultural heritage and enduring beauty of this ancient craft from Yunnan.

programs.title: Grand Song of the Dong — Natural Oriental Harmony
programs.desc:
The Grand Song of the Dong is a polyphonic folk singing tradition of the Dong ethnic group. Performers from several villages gather together to sing, unaccompanied.
The songs, which describe the history, beliefs, customs, and emotions of the Dong people, are an important part of their cultural identity and contribute significantly to their social cohesion.
Generally sung in the Dong language, which has no written form, they are passed on orally from one generation to the next. Classified into various genres—including narrative and love songs, drinking and funeral songs—they are often accompanied by clapping, stomping, and dancing.
Sung on occasions such as festivals, weddings, and funerals, the Grand Song, as a valuable part of the Dong ethnic group’s cultural heritage, reflects their wisdom and creativity, as well as their harmony with nature.
